item.title: fifawc - CLI app to track FIFA World Cup 2018
item.author: nishchayparashar98@gmail.com (Nishchay Parashar)
item.description: fifawc A small CLI app to track FIFA World Cup 2018. About fifawc is a CLI app written in Go to help developers keep track of the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2018 without leaving their terminals. The app uses football-data.org API to provide data. Installation Pre-requisite - Install Go - Set PATH variable Get and Install fifawc app with the command: > go get -u github.com/nishchayp/fifawc To run fifawc app from across the system copy fifawc binary to directory pointed by PATH environmental variable.

item.title: Git State of Mystery
item.author: nishchayparashar98@gmail.com (Nishchay Parashar)
item.description: Explaination of the three states As teams get bigger and projects more complex there arises a need for tools which can keep track of all the changes made to the project by each team member, allow each member to work on their copies of the project simultaneously without breaking the project for each other, help collaborate and combine the changes made by different members, rollback certain changes and a whole lot more, all for the sake of efficient workflow of the project and effective collaboration between team members.

item.author: nishchayparashar98@gmail.com (Nishchay Parashar)
item.description: Hi, I’m Nishchay Parashar A undergrad student presently persuing my engineering degree with major in Computer Science & Engineering along with a minor in Big Data. I enjoy working with other humans and am presently leading a community of 2500+ student on campus as General Secretary of the CS club of my university. I love attending and conducting tech talks, meet-ups and workshops. I also presently am the GitHub Campus Expert selected by GitHub Education for my university (contact me if you need help with organizing a tech event in your university).
